description: Verify and harden ApiSchema validation, loading, and effective-schema normalization for optional `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries`.
acceptance-criteria: The ApiSchema JSON schema accepts optional `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries` alongside `resources` and `descriptors` without requiring the property.
No `apiSchemaVersion` bump is introduced for the OpenAPI-only `changeQueries` payload.
The raw loaded ApiSchema nodes used by metadata serving preserve `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries` when it is present.

The normalized/effective schema nodes used for hashing, model derivation, DDL generation, and mapping-pack selection continue to strip `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments` from core and extension schemas.
Adding, removing, or changing only OpenAPI payloads, including `changeQueries`, does not change the effective-schema hash.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/ApiSchema/JsonSchemaForApiSchema.json` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Startup/ApiSchemaInputNormalizer.cs` for the accepted and stripped paths.

Add or update NUnit coverage in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/ApiSchema/ApiSchemaValidatorTests.cs`, `Startup/ApiSchemaInputNormalizerTests.cs`, and `Startup/EffectiveSchemaHashProviderTests.cs`.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaValidatorTests|FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaInputNormalizerTests|FullyQualifiedName~EffectiveSchemaHashProviderTests"`.
Run `dotnet csharpier format src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit`.

description: Add a core OpenAPI service path for the standalone Change-Queries document sourced only from the selected core ApiSchema.
acceptance-criteria: `OpenApiDocument` or an equivalent core helper can return a nullable standalone Change-Queries OpenAPI document from the raw loaded core `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries` node.
The standalone document is not synthesized when the core ApiSchema lacks `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries`.
A present Change-Queries document that passes existing ApiSchema JSON schema validation is treated as present even if it lacks an `/availableChangeVersions` path.
Extension-side `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries` documents are ignored for standalone metadata, including when the core document is absent.
Standalone document retrieval uses the raw ApiSchema nodes rather than normalized/effective schema nodes that intentionally strip `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments`.
Returned documents are deep-cloned before endpoint-specific mutation so servers and security injection do not mutate the raw loaded ApiSchema or cached resource/descriptor specifications.
steps-to-verify: Review and update `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/OpenApi/OpenApiDocument.cs`, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/OpenApi/OpenApiDocumentTypes.cs` if a new OpenAPI support type is needed, `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/ApiService.cs`, and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Interface/IApiService.cs`.
Add or update NUnit coverage in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/OpenApi/OpenApiDocumentTests.cs` and any existing ApiService metadata tests.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~OpenApiDocumentTests|FullyQualifiedName~ApiService"`.
Inspect tests to confirm resource and descriptor OpenAPI generation still uses the existing resource/descriptor paths.

description: Expose the canonical Change-Queries metadata URL and conditionally list it from `/metadata/specifications`.
acceptance-criteria: `GET /metadata/changequeries/v1/swagger.json` returns the standalone Change-Queries OpenAPI document when the selected core ApiSchema contains `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries`.
`GET /metadata/changequeries/v1/swagger.json` returns `404` when the selected core ApiSchema does not contain a Change-Queries OpenAPI document.
`/metadata/specifications` includes a `Change-Queries` entry only when the core Change-Queries document is present.
The `Change-Queries` specification entry has prefix `Other` and an `endpointUri` pointing to `/metadata/changequeries/v1/swagger.json`.
This story does not add or require the compatibility alias `/metadata/specifications/changequeries-spec.json`.
steps-to-verify: Review and update `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Modules/MetadataEndpointModule.cs`.
Add or update NUnit coverage in `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/Modules/MetaDataModuleTests.cs` for present and absent Change-Queries metadata.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~MetaDataModuleTests"`.

Manually inspect the route table or endpoint tests to confirm existing resources, descriptors, discovery, profile metadata routes, and the absence of `/metadata/specifications/changequeries-spec.json` compatibility alias behavior still match story scope.

description: Refactor OpenAPI server URL and security injection so the standalone Change-Queries document uses the Change Queries runtime base while resource and descriptor documents keep the data API base.
acceptance-criteria: Resource, descriptor, and profile OpenAPI documents continue to receive a `servers` URL ending in `/data`.
The standalone Change-Queries OpenAPI document receives a `servers` URL ending in `/changeQueries/v1`.
Change-Queries server URL generation preserves the same tenant placeholders, route-qualifier placeholders, defaults, and enum values used by resource and descriptor metadata.
The standalone Change-Queries document receives the same OpenAPI 3 OAuth2 `components.securitySchemes.oauth2_client_credentials` entry as resources and descriptors.
The standalone Change-Queries document receives the same root `security` requirement as resources and descriptors.
steps-to-verify: Review and update `MetadataEndpointModule.GetServers` or introduce a scoped equivalent that accepts the OpenAPI route base.
Review and update `ApiService` security injection so standalone Change-Queries metadata shares the existing OAuth2 behavior.
Add or update frontend unit tests for server URL generation with no tenancy, multi-tenancy, route qualifiers, and multi-tenancy plus route qualifiers.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~MetaDataModuleTests"`.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ApiService"`.

description: Add the Change Queries runtime URL to root Discovery API responses independently of standalone OpenAPI document presence.
acceptance-criteria: Root Discovery API responses include `urls.changeQueries`.
`urls.changeQueries` ends in `/changeQueries/v1/` with a trailing slash.
`urls.changeQueries` uses the same PathBase, tenant, and route-qualifier prefix behavior as `urls.dataManagementApi`.
`urls.changeQueries` is emitted regardless of whether the selected core ApiSchema contains `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries`.
Existing discovery URL entries keep their current names and route behavior.
steps-to-verify: Review and update `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Modules/DiscoveryEndpointModule.cs`.
Update `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/Modules/DiscoveryModuleTests.cs` expectations, including URL counts and route-qualifier cases.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DiscoveryModuleTests"`.
If E2E discovery assertions include exact `urls` payloads, update the relevant feature expectations without adding runtime Change Query behavior assertions.

description: Ensure resource and descriptor OpenAPI metadata preserves MetaEd-emitted Change Query paths and live ChangeVersion filters while applying existing path-domain filtering.
acceptance-criteria: Resource and descriptor metadata preserve MetaEd-emitted live GET-many `minChangeVersion` and `maxChangeVersion` parameters.

Resource and descriptor metadata preserve MetaEd-emitted `/deletes` and `/keyChanges` paths for core resources, extension-defined resources, descriptors, and `SchoolYearType` when those paths are present in the ApiSchema OpenAPI fragments.
No runtime route resolution uses OpenAPI paths as the source of truth for `/availableChangeVersions`, `/deletes`, or `/keyChanges`.
`DomainsExcludedFromOpenApi` applies to MetaEd-emitted `/deletes` and `/keyChanges` paths by the same path-level `x-Ed-Fi-domains` rules used for live resource and descriptor paths.
Domain filtering preserves the existing multi-domain behavior: a path is removed only when all valid path domains are excluded.
Domain-filtered tags and component cleanup do not remove schemas that are still referenced by remaining Change Query paths.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/OpenApi/OpenApiDocument.cs` path and tag filtering behavior against MetaEd-emitted `/deletes` and `/keyChanges` fragments.

Add or update unit tests in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/OpenApi/OpenApiDocumentTests.cs` for live parameters, Change Query paths, extension fragments, descriptors, and excluded-domain behavior.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~OpenApiDocumentTests"`.
Review any generated OpenAPI sample fixture used by tests to confirm it is MetaEd-style input rather than DMS-synthesized Change Query paths.

description: Update profile OpenAPI filtering so readable profiled resources keep their Change Query paths without rewriting tracked-change response schemas into profile-shaped resource schemas.
acceptance-criteria: Profile resource OpenAPI documents preserve live GET-many `minChangeVersion` and `maxChangeVersion` parameters for readable profiled resources.
Profile resource OpenAPI documents include `/deletes` and `/keyChanges` paths when the corresponding base resource path is readable in the profile.
Profile association for `/deletes` and `/keyChanges` is based on the profiled base resource endpoint path, not the tracked-change response schema name.
Profile `/deletes` and `/keyChanges` responses remain `application/json` Change Query responses and do not use profile-specific media types.
Tracked-change key schemas are not filtered by normal readable-resource property rules and are not rewritten to `_readable` schema variants.
The standalone `Change-Queries` document for `/availableChangeVersions` is not listed or served under profile metadata.
Paths removed by `DomainsExcludedFromOpenApi` from the base resource specification are not reintroduced by profile OpenAPI generation.
steps-to-verify: Review and update `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/OpenApi/ProfileOpenApiSpecificationFilter.cs`.
Add or update unit tests for `ProfileOpenApiSpecificationFilter` covering readable resources with live paths, `/deletes`, `/keyChanges`, tracked-change schemas, and excluded-domain base specs.
Run `dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ProfileOpenApiSpecificationFilter"`.

Run `dotnet test src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~MetaDataModuleTests"` for profile metadata endpoint coverage.

description: Add focused end-to-end metadata coverage using realistic ApiSchema inputs for Change-Queries OpenAPI, missing-document behavior, extension ignoring, and discovery URL exposure.
acceptance-criteria: A test fixture with core `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries` proves the canonical metadata route serves the raw standalone document with server and security injection.
A fixture without core `changeQueries` proves startup or app initialization succeeds, `/metadata/specifications` omits `Change-Queries`, and `/metadata/changequeries/v1/swagger.json` returns `404`.
A fixture with extension-only `projectSchema.openApiBaseDocuments.changeQueries` proves extension-side standalone documents are ignored.
Tests verify `urls.changeQueries` in Discovery API output with the same route prefix behavior as `urls.dataManagementApi`.
Tests stay metadata-focused and do not assert runtime `/changeQueries/v1/availableChangeVersions`, `/deletes`, or `/keyChanges` endpoint semantics owned by neighboring stories.
steps-to-verify: Prefer fast frontend or core integration-style tests before adding Docker E2E coverage; only add DMS E2E scenarios if existing metadata coverage cannot exercise configured ApiSchema inputs.
If DMS E2E coverage is added, follow the repo E2E setup instructions in `AGENTS.md` and use `./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest` for relational-backend shards when required.
Run the targeted unit and integration test projects touched by the implementation.
Run `dotnet csharpier format src/dms/core src/dms/frontend src/dms/tests` after test edits.
